sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for SNES port

special_mechanics.name: turn-based_real-time_hybrid
special_mechanics.description: Ultima VI incorporated some real-time elements into the traditional turn-based RPG formula, affecting movement and combat flow

special_mechanics.name: tile_based_movement
special_mechanics.description: Game uses seamless 1024x1024 tile map with integrated towns and dungeons in single scale overhead view

special_mechanics.name: party_movement
special_mechanics.description: Avatar controls party with multiple companions (Shamino, Dupre, Iolo) with synchronized movement

tier_notes: Tier 3 empirical/qualitative data. Ultima VI SNES is an RPG with minimal documented movement physics. Wikipedia source covers gameplay design philosophy (real-time elements, tile-based movement, oblique perspective) but contains no quantitative physics parameters. No technical ROM documentation available for SNES port specifics.
sources_used: wikipedia - Ultima VI: The False Prophet (gameplay and development sections)
